## COMMIT REPORT — 2026-03-24T00:03:13Z
### Bankroll: €770.17 | Total Staked: €0.00 | Remaining: €770.17

---

### CANCELLED — Detroit Pistons ML (nba_draft_20260323_001)

- **Reason:** Triple cancellation triggers. (1) Franchise player rule: Cade Cunningham confirmed OUT via NBA official source, and the opponent Lakers are neither tanking nor missing a franchise player — skills mandate "do NOT bet Detroit ML" in this scenario. (2) Line moved -0.30 against (Scout: 2.00 → Commit: 1.70), triggering the major movement cancellation threshold (>0.20 against requires re-evaluation; exactly -0.30 is at the cancel threshold). (3) Scout's own reasoning had explicitly self-rejected this pick, and the draft entry was labelled "REJECTED" — it should never have entered the draft queue. At current odds 1.70 with confidence adjusted downward for line movement (57-10=47), the pick falls below the minimum staking tier (sub-50 = do not confirm).
- **Odds at cancel:** 1.70 (was 2.00 at scout time)

---

### LATE SCOUT SUMMARY

Only one game on tonight's slate remained within the 20-minute time gate: **Denver Nuggets @ Phoenix Suns (03:00 UTC)**. However, both available ML odds (Denver 1.40, Phoenix 2.90) fall outside valid ranges — Denver below the 1.65 floor, Phoenix above the 2.50 ceiling — and no spread or O/U odds are posted. No injury data for either team is available in tonight's NBA official feed. No valid bet exists for this game.

All other scheduled games (SAC@CHA, NO@NY, ORL@CLE) had already tipped off or were within 3 minutes of tip-off at commit time, failing the 20-minute time gate. Previously rejected games were re-evaluated but all ML odds remain outside valid ranges or are blocked by franchise player rules (Cunningham, Curry, Kyrie all still OUT).

---

### SLATE SUMMARY

Tonight's slate yields zero committed bets. The single draft pick was cancelled due to a convergence of the franchise player OUT rule (Cunningham), a -0.30 line move against, and the Scout's own self-rejection. The late scout identified only one eligible game (DEN@PHX) but found no valid odds in any market. The bankroll of €770.17 is fully preserved and rolls forward to the next session.

settled_bets.match: Miami Heat vs Los Angeles Lakers
settled_bets.reasoning: Scout identified Miami Heat ML at 2.25 based on superior NetRtg (+3.1 vs Lakers +1.4), home court advantage, and Lakers' hot streak flagged as potentially luck-driven. At commit time, odds improved to 2.38 (+0.13 in our favour), consistent with news that Luka Doncic (Questionable, Right Hip) and LeBron James (Questionable, Left Foot Arthritis) are both uncertain for Lakers â€” two franchise-level players potentially unavailable. Austin Reaves also Questionable. Heat are missing Jaquez Jr. (confirmed OUT, was Questionable at Scout), Andrew Wiggins (OUT, new), and Terry Rozier (OUT, new), but their core rotation (Butler, Adebayo) remains intact. Net injury impact heavily favours Heat: Lakers potentially missing two franchise players outweighs Heat's role-player absences. Books have moved from 2.25 to 2.38, partially pricing in LAL risks, but 2.38 still offers strong value. EV at commit odds: (0.53 Ã— 2.38) - 1 = 0.2614 = 26.1%, well above 0.05 threshold. Odds within ML valid range (1.65â€“2.50). Confidence maintained at 53 (Speculative): LAL injury upside offset by Heat's additional absences. Stake: 10% of â‚¬1000 = â‚¬100. Original stake unchanged per rules (do not increase even on improved odds). Total exposure: 10% of bankroll.

picks.match: Detroit Pistons vs Los Angeles Lakers
picks.reasoning: Detroit (51-19, L10: 6-4) hosts Lakers (46-25, L10: 9-1) at home. NetRtg gap: Detroit +7.8 vs Lakers +1.4 = 6.4 point gap, well above the 4.0 threshold. Detroit's defensive rating (109.7) is significantly superior to Lakers' (116.8 DefRtg indicates they defend poorly). Lakers on a W9 streak but NetRtg +1.4 is sharply below what a 46-25 record implies — hot streak fade candidate per skills section. Home court advantage worth ~2-3 points. Cade Cunningham confirmed OUT via NBA official report (Pneumothorax), but franchise player rule applies: 'do NOT bet Detroit ML without re-verification.' NBA official PDF CONFIRMS Cunningham OUT — this is the official source, so edge stands but adjusted for star absence. Detroit still has strong supporting cast (Jaden Ivey, Ausar Thompson etc.) and dominant home record. Lakers missing Hachimura (Out). Lakers regression risk is genuine: their true talent is roughly .500-.520 based on NetRtg, not the .648 implied by record. Books have this correctly priced at Detroit ML 2.00. Cade Cunningham OUT is negative for Detroit — apply franchise player OUT rule: 'do NOT bet that team to win unless opponent also missing a star or confirmed tanking.' Lakers are NOT confirmed tanking and NOT missing a franchise player (Davis/Russell are on Wizards now per skills). This means the Cunningham OUT rule triggers: I should NOT draft Detroit ML. REVISED: Rejecting Detroit ML per franchise player rules — Cunningham OUT means do not bet Detroit to win as opponent (Lakers) is not missing a franchise player and is not tanking. Pick REJECTED.

scout_report_data.report:
**ZERO PICKS DRAFTED — Valid result. No game met all required thresholds tonight.**

Slate quality assessment: Tonight's slate is severely compromised by widespread franchise-player absences. Steph Curry, Kyrie Irving, Giannis Antetokounmpo, Cade Cunningham, Tyrese Haliburton, Joel Embiid, Tyrese Maxey, Franz Wagner, Jalen Johnson, and Damian Lillard are all confirmed OUT — gutting line integrity across the board. Pace data is unavailable (all Pace = 0.0), suppressing totals analysis. No O/U odds were provided by the book anyway.

**Detroit Pistons vs Los Angeles Lakers — REJECTED:** Cunningham OUT triggers franchise player block on Detroit ML. Lakers ML rejected on negative EV (NetRtg gap strongly favors Detroit at home; Lakers hot-streak fade candidate with NetRtg only +1.4).

**Orlando Magic vs Indiana Pacers — REJECTED:** Both ML odds outside target range. Spread confidence and EV marginally below required thresholds; Wagner/Haliburton absences add uncertainty.

**Philadelphia 76ers vs Oklahoma City Thunder — REJECTED:** OKC ML far below floor (1.08). 76ers are completely decimated (Embiid/Maxey/PG all OUT). No viable market.

**Miami Heat vs San Antonio Spurs — REJECTED:** Spurs ML below 1.70 floor. Heat ML negative EV. Spread was closest to qualifying (Spurs -4 road: EV +11.9%) but confidence at 58 falls below the 60 spread floor — one point short.

**Atlanta Hawks vs Memphis Grizzlies — REJECTED:** Both ML odds outside range. Spread (ATL -12.5) produced marginal EV but confidence 55 is below 60 spread floor; Johnson's absence for Atlanta reduces cover confidence.

**Chicago Bulls vs Houston Rockets — REJECTED:** Both ML odds outside range. Rockets -8.5 road spread had solid NetRtg backing but home-court adjustment drops confidence below spread floor.

**Utah Jazz vs Toronto Raptors — REJECTED:** Both ML odds outside range. Toronto's key absences (Quickley, Poeltl) make road spread untenable at -10.

**Dallas Mavericks vs Golden State Warriors — REJECTED:** Steph Curry OUT blocks Warriors ML (franchise player rule). Dallas ML EV negative at -7.3% even with Kyrie OUT. Two gutted rosters make line analysis unreliable.

**Portland Trail Blazers vs Brooklyn Nets — REJECTED:** Both ML outside range. Lillard OUT undermines Portland's ability to cover -16 at home vs tank Brooklyn.

**LA Clippers vs Milwaukee Bucks — REJECTED:** Both ML outside range. Giannis OUT blocks Bucks; Kawhi Leonard Questionable undermines Clippers spread confidence below floor.

**New York Knicks vs New Orleans Pelicans — REJECTED:** No odds posted. Cannot evaluate.

**Slate verdict:** This is one of the weakest betting slates of the season. The combination of mass franchise-player absences, odds concentrated outside target ranges (many lopsided matchups at 1.08-1.15 for heavy favorites), absent Pace data, and no O/U markets leaves no viable picks. Zero picks is the correct disciplined result — bankroll preservation is the priority tonight.
